content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814


July 16; 1976


Mr. William Bennett
Chairman
State Board of Equalization
1020 N Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Timber Advisory Committee

Dear Mr. Bennett:

This is in reference to your letter of June 10, 1976. We
apologize for such a delayed response.

At its most recent meeting on July 8, the Fair Political
Practices Commission undertook a reevaluation of its regulation
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a) defining "member of a decision-
making body." There was strong sentiment among some Commission
members that subsection (a)(1)(C) of the regulation was resulting
in the unwarranted inclusion of advisory board members within
conflict of interest codes. The matter will be further discussed
at meetings scheduled for July 20 and early August. At that time,
there is substantial likelihood that Section 18700(a)(1)(C) will
be eliminated.

In light of these developments, I will not attempt to
render final advice until the Commission's early August meeting.
In the interim, my advice is that even if Timber Advisory
Committee members are, for the time being, to be deemed "public
officials" by virtue of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(1)(C),
their participation in matters which will have a general effect
on the timber industry or the subgroups of that industry which
you cite, is permitted by virtue of the Commission's regulation
defining "effect on the public generally," 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Section 18703(d).

I hope this informal advice will assist you in resolving
the matter, at least on an interim basis. If you have further
questions, please call Kenneth Goshorn of our Conflict of
Interest division at 322-6444. Thank you for writing.

Sincerely,

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION
1020 N STREET, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA
(P.O. BOX 1799, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95808)

(916) 445-4081

GEORGE R. REILLY
First District, San Francisco

JOHN W. LYNCH
Second District, Fresno

WILLIAM M. BENNETT
Third District, San Rafael

RICHARD NEVINS
Fourth District, Pasadena

KENNETH CORY
Controller, Sacramento

W. W. DUNLOP
Executive Secretary

June 10, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100-Kay Street
Sacramento, California  95814

Attention:  Mr. Michael Bennett
           Executive Director

Dear Mr. Bennett:

     This Board is required by the Statutes of 1976, Chapter
176, commonly referred to as the Timber Yield Tax Law, to appoint
a "timber advisory committee". Please provide us with an official
ruling as to whether or not the members of this standing committee
are subject to the "Political Reform Act of 1974".

     Pursuant to Section 431(c) of the Revenue and Taxation
Code the committee is to be composed of one member of this Board,
one representative of the Board of Forestry, five county assessors,
one member representing small-scale timber owners, and one member
representing large-scale timber owners. The Board is required by
Sections 434 through 434.5 of the Revenue Code to consult with the
committee prior to preparing temporary, and eventually permanent
rules setting forth criteria and procedures for grading timberland
on the basis of its site quality and operability. The criteria and
procedures are to be used by all assessors to grade timberland for
tax purposes. The Board must additionally, following consultation
with the committee, adopt rules containing schedules of values which
the assessors must use as the value of property graded pursuant to
Board's site classification rules.

     Although the elected and state employee members of the
committee have responsibilities under the Political Reform Act, we
are uncertain concerning their responsibilities as members and, more
particularly, the appropriate interpretation of sections 18700 and
18703 of Article 1 of Chapter 7 of Title 2 in Division VI of the
California Administrative Code.

     Since the law requires us to take action in the very near
future, we would appreciate your giving this matter priority. Your
decision could have substantial impact on our ability to obtain
industry representatives. Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

WMB:el                                    William M. Bennett
cc  Hon. George R. Reilly                 Chairman
    Hon. Iris Sankey
    Hon. Richard Nevins              Mr. C. H. Otterman
    Hon. Kenneth Cory                Mr. W. W. Dunlop

sections.conclusion: In light of these developments, I will not attempt to render final advice until the Commission's early August meeting. In the interim, my advice is that even if Timber Advisory Committee members are, for the time being, to be deemed "public officials" by virtue of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(1)(C), their participation in matters which will have a general effect on the timber industry, or the subgroups of that industry which you represent, is permitted by virtue of the Commission's regulation defining "effect on the public generally," 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18703(d).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether members of the Timber Advisory Committee established under the Timber Yield Tax Law are subject to the Political Reform Act of 1974, and how sections 18700 and 18703 of the California Administrative Code apply to their participation in committee decisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC will provide final advice after its August meeting, but interim guidance permits Timber Advisory Committee members to participate in matters affecting the timber industry generally under the "effect on the public generally" exception, even if they are deemed public officials.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with some formatting issues; contains both the incoming request (June 10, 1976) and FPPC response letter (July 16, 1976); FPPC was reconsidering Section 18700(a)(1)(C) regarding advisory board member definitions at the time of response.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the State Board of Equalization's inquiry about whether Timber Advisory Committee members are subject to the Political Reform Act, providing interim advice that permits their participation in industry matters affecting the public generally while deferring final guidance pending a regulatory reevaluation scheduled for August.
